title: gutierrezia leucantha
intro: Gutierrezia leucantha, also known as white clackbrush or white snakeweed, is a shrubby perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, belonging to the Asteraceae family. The plant's leaves and stems have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Gutierrezia leucantha include its potential to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and cold, as well as its ability to aid in wound healing and reduce inflammation. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems are often infused in hot water to make a tea, or dried and used as a powder or capsule for its medicinal benefits.
